original_abstract: In this study, Holmqvist offers an original and incisive analysis of the shifting relationship between warfare and policing in the twenty-first century. By exploring contemporary Western strategic thought through an interdisciplinary lens, Holmqvist reveals how the traditional boundaries between military action and domestic law enforcement have become increasingly blurred. The work challenges established paradigms, arguing that modern security strategies reflect a fundamental transformation in how global order is maintained and contested in an era of transnational threats.
humanized_abstract: Modern war and modern policing: New relationships and older traditions is a study of changing warfare and policing relations in the twenty-first century. It presents an innovative analysis of the Western strategic discourse and thereby sheds light on present and potential futures of global security governance by applying an interdisciplinary approach. Warfare and policing are mutually exclusive, traditional ways of using coercion to enforce decisions and rule over the use of force in society. Holmqvist uncovers and examines the borders between these two mutual exclusives by analysing contemporary Western strategic discourses by using an interdisciplinary approach. This study challenges existing views on current security governance and the constitution of the global order and the ways in which this order is contested and defended in the face of emerging threats and in a context of increasingly asymmetric forms of warfare and counter-terrorism, and in which non-state entities as well as multinational companies and corporations play an increasingly large role.
